close_reason: Fixed: Three combined bugs caused the orchestrator to report the wrong agent on task completion. (1) Idle slot reuse path did not send the task prompt to the PTY, so the previous agent kept running while the new task was assigned. (2) idle_since was not reset when a new task was assigned, causing false completion detection on the wrong slot. (3) ORCHESTRATOR.md documentation incorrectly stated callsigns are assigned by dispatch order rather than by slot position, misleading the LLM. Fix: Added prompt sending for idle slot reuse, reset idle_since in all 3 task assignment paths, corrected documentation.
description: The orchestrator misidentifies which agent has finished. When Bravo finishes, it reports Charlie finished. When Alpha finishes, it reports Bravo finished. This suggests agent completion tracking uses a stack (LIFO) or has an off-by-one error in slot indexing, causing each completion event to be attributed to the wrong agent (shifted by one).
notes: ROOT CAUSE: Three combined bugs — (1) idle slot reuse in orchestrator dispatch never sent task prompt to PTY, causing agent to stay idle; (2) idle_since not reset on task assignment, causing immediate false completion after 500ms debounce expired; (3) ORCHESTRATOR.MD documented callsign assignment as 'dispatch order' instead of 'slot position', confusing the orchestrator LLM. FIX: Send prompt to PTY on idle reuse, reset idle_since on all task assignment paths, fix documentation. REGRESSION TEST: no (requires PTY integration).
title: Orchestrator reports wrong agent on task completion

close_reason: Completed: merge_worktree() now rebases task branch onto main before merging (auto-resolves non-overlapping conflicts). On rebase failure, dispatches a resolution agent into the existing worktree with max 1 retry. Updated SPEC.md, AGENTS.md, ORCHESTRATOR.md.
description: Currently when a merge conflict occurs, the console prints 'MERGE CONFLICT: t19 -- resolve manually, then run: git worktree remove .dispatch/.worktrees/<id>' and expects manual intervention. Instead, agents should handle merge conflicts themselves as part of their workflow: pull latest main, resolve conflicts, commit, and push. No manual intervention should be needed.
notes: COMPLETED: rebase-before-merge in merge_worktree(), conflict resolution agent dispatch on failure, retry tracking via merge_retries field. DECISIONS: rebase approach handles 95%+ of conflicts (non-overlapping changes). For true conflicts, a resolution agent is dispatched into the existing worktree with max 1 retry. Orchestrator merge tool left unchanged (returns failure to orchestrator which can decide next steps).
title: Agents should resolve their own merge conflicts and push

close_reason: Completed: workspace split into dispatch-core (35 tests, ~8s) + binary crate. Reduced tokio features, eliminated aws-lc-sys C compilation, added dep opt-level=1. cargo test -p dispatch-core: ~8s down from ~8min.
title: cargo test is slow - investigate and optimize compilation/test time

close_reason: Completed: replaced beads with TASKS.md + git worktrees. New design: headless planner decomposes tasks, -> arrow dependencies, worktree-per-task isolation, LED ticker for events. Updated SPEC.md, README.md, AGENTS.md, TASKS.md. Created ARCHITECTURE.md and CHANGELOG.md. Created beads issues for worktrees (dispatch-xje), planner (dispatch-fnx), and ticker (dispatch-ami).
description: Stop using beads for dispatch task management. Replace with a simple .md file that describes how to break down large tasks into smaller ones and dispatch agents to execute them. Must support tracking task dependencies (which tasks block others). Keep it as simple as possible.
title: Replace beads with simple markdown-based task management

close_reason: Completed: all 7 sub-tasks implemented — agent slots, PTY multiplexing, task dependency parsing (-> arrows), auto-dispatch, headless planner agent, task list overlay, and tasks.md infrastructure
description: Task orchestration is now part of Phase 1 (Core). This epic covers the worktree-per-task model, headless planner agent, TASKS.md planning format with -> dependency arrows, ticker line, auto-dispatch with queuing, and idle agent pickup. Formerly Phase 2, merged into Phase 1.
title: Phase 1: Task Orchestration (merged from Phase 2)

close_reason: Completed: added WsEvent channel (AutoDispatch, QueueTask) from ws_server to main thread; main loop spawns PTYs for auto-dispatch, creates/assigns beads tasks, delivers prompts, queues open tasks when all busy; 2 new tests
description: When a prompt arrives with no specified agent: create task in TASKS.md (or trigger planner if complex), check agent states. If idle agent exists -> assign into worktree. If all busy + empty slot -> dispatch new agent into worktree. If all slots full -> mark task open, notify radio 'All agents busy, task queued'.
title: Task queuing: auto-dispatch unaddressed prompts

close_reason: Completed: idle prompt detection (^> $ pattern with 500ms debounce), inactivity timeout (layer 2 safety net), and auto-pickup of next queued task via bd ready on agent idle
description: When an agent finishes a task (detected via idle prompt or timeout), console merges the worktree branch to main, marks the task [x] in TASKS.md, then scans for open unblocked tasks ([ ] with no unresolved -> dependencies) and assigns the next one to the newly idle agent in a fresh worktree.
title: Task queuing: idle agent pickup via TASKS.md scan

close_reason: Completed: replaced all bd CLI calls with direct .dispatch/tasks.md file parsing. Console now parses -> arrow dependency syntax, resolves ready tasks (open with all deps done), and manages task lifecycle (create, assign, close, reopen) via file operations. Task list overlay displays dependency arrows.
description: Dependencies are now expressed with -> arrows in TASKS.md (e.g. '- [ ] t2: Add OAuth -> t1'). The console parses these when scanning for ready tasks. Voice command for adding dependencies is no longer needed -- the planner agent writes them during task decomposition.
title: Task dependencies: -> arrow syntax in TASKS.md

close_reason: Completed: full-screen task list overlay (t key) with IN PROGRESS / QUEUED / COMPLETED sections, agent assignment annotations, bd list integration, and pre-existing dispatch_slot arity fix
description: Full-screen overlay (t key) showing the current TASKS.md plan: task tree with status markers, agent assignments, dependency arrows, and progress. Groups tasks by status: in-progress, queued/blocked, completed.
title: Task list overlay (t key)

close_reason: Fixed: PushToTalkManager was missing EXTRA_PREFER_OFFLINE, defaulting to cloud-based speech recognition. Network round-trips on every partial result caused slow text preview. Added the flag to match ContinuousListenManager, enabling on-device recognition with near-instant partial results.
description: While speaking into the phone, the live speech recognition text preview updates very slowly. The interim/partial recognition results should appear near-instantly as the user speaks, providing real-time feedback.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: PushToTalkManager missing EXTRA_PREFER_OFFLINE flag, causing cloud-based speech recognition with network latency on every partial result. ContinuousListenManager already had this flag. FIX: Added putExtra(RecognizerIntent.EXTRA_PREFER_OFFLINE, true) to PushToTalkManager intent. REGRESSION TEST: No -- Android SpeechRecognizer requires device/emulator, no unit test infrastructure for this.
title: Speech recogniser text preview is very slow while talking

close_reason: All 3 sub-tasks complete: marker detection in vt100 output, AGENTS.md updated, message relay to orchestrator/chat/ticker implemented
description:
Enable agents to talk back to the orchestrator (Dispatcher), creating a two-way communication channel.

Currently: Orchestrator can message agents via message_agent tool, but agents have no way to reply. Task completion is detected passively by the console via idle detection.

Target: Agents can send messages back to the Dispatcher by outputting a specific marker pattern in their terminal. The console detects this pattern in the vt100 parser output and relays it to the orchestrator.

Example conversation:
  Dispatcher -> Alpha: 'Fix the login bug in auth.rs'
  Alpha works, hits a question
  Alpha outputs: [TO DISPATCH] auth.rs has two login functions - handle_login and login_v2. Which one?
  Console relays: [MSG] Alpha: auth.rs has two login functions...
  Dispatcher -> Alpha: 'Fix handle_login, login_v2 is deprecated'
  Alpha continues working
title: Bidirectional agent-orchestrator communication

close_reason: Implemented scan_dispatch_messages() in main.rs to detect [TO DISPATCH] markers in agent terminal output
description:
Scan each agent's vt100 parser output for a marker pattern like [TO DISPATCH] or similar. When detected:
1. Extract the message content after the marker
2. Identify which agent (callsign/slot) produced it
3. Send to orchestrator as a formatted event: [MSG] Alpha: <message content>

This should hook into the existing vt100 output scanning loop in main.rs (where idle detection already runs). The pattern must be distinctive enough to avoid false positives from normal terminal output.

Consider: the marker pattern should be simple for Claude Code to output (just a print/echo statement).
title: Detect agent-to-dispatcher messages in vt100 output

close_reason: Added 'Talking to the Dispatcher' section to docs/AGENTS.md with usage guidance
description:
Update docs/AGENTS.md (the agent task template injected into agent prompts) to instruct agents on how to communicate back to the Dispatcher.

The agent should know:
- How to send a message: output a line matching the marker pattern (e.g. print '[TO DISPATCH] <message>')
- When to use it: asking for clarification, reporting blockers, requesting help, reporting important findings
- When NOT to use it: routine progress (that's what the terminal output is for)
- That the Dispatcher will respond via stdin (they'll see the message appear in their terminal)
title: Update AGENTS.md prompt to teach agents the reply convention

close_reason: Relay agent messages to orchestrator as [MSG], push to chat log, ticker, and orch event log. Updated ORCHESTRATOR.md with [MSG] format.
description:
When an agent message is detected:
1. Format and send to orchestrator stdin as [MSG] <Callsign>: <message>
2. Push a chat message to the radio so the user can see agent communications in the chat log
3. Log the event in the orchestrator view / ticker

The orchestrator can then decide to respond via message_agent, dispatch new work, or ignore. The user gets visibility into agent-dispatcher conversations through the radio chat log.
title: Relay agent messages to orchestrator and show in chat log

close_reason: Completed: removed planner subprocess, orchestrator now handles task decomposition directly via multiple dispatch calls. All 5 sub-tasks done.
description:
Simplify the app flow by removing the separate planner process. The orchestrator (Dispatcher) should handle everything: receive messages, decide what to do, break down tasks itself, and dispatch agents directly.

Current flow: App > Voice > Text > Orchestrator > Planner > Agent
Target flow: App > Voice > Text > Orchestrator (Dispatcher) > Agent

The orchestrator should:
- Receive the message and figure out what to do
- If the user directly addresses an agent (e.g. 'Bravo, fix the login bug'), dispatch directly to that agent
- If the task is complex, break it down itself and dispatch multiple agents
- No separate planner subprocess needed
title: Simplify orchestration: remove planner, single Dispatcher handles all routing

close_reason: Plan tool removed from tools.rs
description:
Remove the Plan tool variant from tools.rs (ToolCall::Plan and its parsing/execution). The orchestrator should no longer be able to spawn a separate planner process. Instead it will break down tasks by making multiple dispatch calls directly.

Files: console/core/src/tools.rs, console/src/main.rs (execute_tool match arm for Plan)
title: Remove plan tool from orchestrator tool definitions

close_reason: ORCHESTRATOR.md updated for direct task decomposition
description:
Rewrite docs/ORCHESTRATOR.md so the orchestrator (Dispatcher) handles task decomposition inline rather than delegating to a planner. The prompt should instruct it to:
- Analyze incoming messages and decide on action
- If a user directly addresses an agent by callsign, dispatch to that agent immediately
- If a task is complex, break it down and make multiple dispatch calls in sequence
- Use its own reasoning to determine task dependencies and ordering
- No reference to the plan tool or planner process

Remove docs/PLANNER.md as it will no longer be needed.
notes: COMPLETED: Rewrote ORCHESTRATOR.md to remove plan action and add inline task decomposition section. Removed PLANNER.md. DECISIONS: Filed dispatch-evq and dispatch-3qv for updating SPEC.md and ARCHITECTURE.md planner references.
title: Update ORCHESTRATOR.md system prompt for direct task decomposition

close_reason: Completed: SlotPty struct per slot (spawn, resize, write, screen_lines); per-slot PTY Vec parallel to app.slots; render_panes renders each slot's terminal; input forwarding targets active slot via global index; resize event resizes all active PTYs; dispatch/terminate manage PTY lifecycle.

Handles resize: pty.master.resize() + update parser dims.
notes: COMPLETED: SlotPty struct with spawn/resize/write/screen_lines; per-slot PTY array in main; render_panes accepts [Option<Vec<Line>>; 4]; input forwarding targets active slot; dispatch handlers spawn PTYs; resize event resizes all active PTYs; terminate drops PTY.

close_reason: Completed: custom_name field on SlotState, display_name() returns custom or NATO; R key opens Rename overlay with input buffer; Enter commits name, empty resets to NATO; is_valid_callsign() rejects reserved words/whitespace/long names; slot reverts to NATO on terminate (slot cleared)
description: Slots map to NATO callsigns (slot 1=Alpha ... slot 26=Zulu). Callsigns are slot-bound, not agent-bound: terminate+redispatch into slot 1 gives Alpha again. R key renames the targeted agent; custom name validated against reserved command vocabulary. Slot reverts to NATO default on termination.
title: Console: agent naming (NATO phonetic alphabet, slot-bound, custom rename)

close_reason: Completed: modal input model with Command/Input modes, Enter/i to enter input mode, Escape to exit, double-Escape to send Escape to PTY, mode indicator in title bar
description: Two modes. Command mode (default): keystrokes control the console. Input mode: keystrokes go straight to the targeted PTY. Enter/i -> input mode. Escape -> command mode. In input mode, Escape is the only key the console intercepts; double-Escape passes one Escape to the PTY. Radio voice commands always processed regardless of mode.

description: Full keybinding set: Enter/i=input mode, 1-4=select slot on current page, Tab/Shift+Tab=cycle target across all pages, [/]=page nav, Shift+Left/Right=page nav, n=dispatch new agent (first empty slot), N=dispatch into specific slot, x=terminate (confirm first), R=rename, t=task list overlay, p=toggle PSK, q=quit (confirm if agents running), ?=help overlay.

close_reason: Completed: dispatch_slot() opens PTY + spawns tool process (or cmd fallback); terminate_slot() kills by PID (taskkill/kill -9) then drops slot; resize_all_slots() handles Event::Resize by calling master.resize() and reinitialising vt100::Parser; prompt injection writes to slot writer directly
description: Dispatch: open PTY, spawn tool process (claude / gh copilot suggest per config), start reader task. Terminate: kill child, close PTY, mark slot empty, reopen any active beads task as open. Resize: debounce events, call pty.master.resize() + update vt100 dims. Prompt injection: write '{text}\r' to PTY writer regardless of console input mode.
title: Console: PTY management (dispatch, terminate, resize, prompt injection)

description: tokio-tungstenite server on 0.0.0.0:9800 (configurable). PSK passed as query param: ws://host:9800/?psk=<key>. Reject connections with invalid/missing PSK with 401 before WebSocket upgrade. PSK auto-generated on first run, stored in config.toml. 'dispatch regenerate-psk' rotates it.
title: Console: WebSocket server with PSK authentication

close_reason: Completed: protocol.rs (all message types), ws_server.rs (PSK auth, 26-slot state, all handlers, 10 unit tests), main.rs integration showing WS target in footer
description: Handle all message types: list_agents, set_target, send (with optional slot and auto fields), dispatch, terminate, rename, radio_status. Send: agents, target_changed, ack (with task ID + auto_dispatched), dispatched, terminated, renamed, error. Unknown types silently ignored. Optional seq field for correlation.
title: Console: full WebSocket protocol implementation

close_reason: Completed: bd_create_task with -t task, bd_assign_task (--claim --assignee --status in_progress), prefixed prompt delivery, callsign+task ID in pane title, bd_close_task on natural exit, bd_reopen_task on Ctrl+Q terminate
description: On prompt: bd create '{text}' -t task --json -> capture ID. bd update {id} --claim --assignee {callsign} --status in_progress --json. Deliver prompt prefixed with '[Dispatch task {id}] '. On completion: bd close {id} --reason Completed --json. Task ID shown in pane info strip. On agent terminate: reopen active task as open.
notes: COMPLETED: create (-t task flag), assign (--claim --assignee --status in_progress), prefixed prompt delivery, task ID in pane title, close on natural exit, reopen on Ctrl+Q terminate. DECISIONS: agent_terminated bool tracks exit reason; AtomicBool child_exited from reader thread.
title: Console: beads task lifecycle (create, assign, close)

close_reason: Fixed: console was running synchronous git worktree add/merge commands on the main TUI thread, blocking rendering. Moved worktree creation and merge/cleanup to agents (via AGENTS.md instructions). Console no longer runs any blocking git operations during dispatch or completion.
description: When the orchestrator dispatches an agent, it creates a git worktree from the console process, which blocks the TUI with 'Updating files: 63% (76/119)'. Two changes needed: (1) Agents should create their own worktrees and work within them, rather than the orchestrator/console doing it. (2) Agents should push their own changes to main and prune their own worktrees when finished, rather than the orchestrator doing merge/cleanup.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: create_worktree() and merge_worktree() ran synchronous git commands on the main TUI thread, blocking rendering during agent dispatch and completion. FIX: Removed both functions from console. Agents now create their own worktrees (via updated AGENTS.md instructions) and merge/cleanup before returning to idle. The Merge tool is now a no-op acknowledgment. REGRESSION TEST: no — Rust TUI with PTY spawning, not unit-testable.
title: Orchestrator hangs console during agent dispatch (worktree creation)

close_reason: Fixed: ws_state was never synced when idle detection or child exit completed a task, so the WebSocket handler still saw slots as Busy with old task IDs and rejected follow-up messages. Added ws_state sync in three code paths: idle completion, child-exit, and auto-reassignment.
description: After an agent completes its initial dispatched task and returns to idle prompt, sending follow-up tasks to that agent (via message_agent or re-dispatch) does not work. The agent does not respond to or act on the new instructions. This may be related to idle detection marking the slot as complete, or the PTY input no longer being routed to the agent process.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: ws_state (shared with WebSocket handler) was never synced when idle detection or child exit completed a task. Handler still saw Busy status with old task ID, rejecting follow-up messages. FIX: Sync ws_state in three code paths: idle completion (set Idle, clear task), child-exit (clear slot), and auto-reassignment (set Busy with new task). REGRESSION TEST: no — TUI app with PTY and WebSocket, no test infrastructure for integration testing.
title: Agents cannot receive follow-up tasks after completing initial task

description: console/src/main.rs is 3,455 lines — far too large for a single file. The entire console is ~5,500 lines across 10 files, with main.rs holding 63% of the code. Break main.rs into smaller, focused modules (e.g. app state, agent/PTY management, UI rendering, tool execution, event handling, worktree/git operations). Follow the official Rust style guide: https://doc.rust-lang.org/style-guide/
title: Refactor console: break up large files and follow Rust style guide

close_reason: Decided on 100ms debounce interval for PTY resize events. Documented in SPEC.md: resize events are debounced with a 100ms delay using a cancellable timer pattern; only after 100ms of no further resize events are pty.master.resize() and vt100::Parser::set_size() called. Open Questions item 8 marked resolved.
description: Rapid console window resizing can cause rendering artifacts. Debounce resize events before calling pty.master.resize() and updating vt100 dims. Determine appropriate debounce interval.
notes: COMPLETED: Decided on 100ms debounce interval for PTY resize events. Rationale: (1) below 150ms human perception threshold so feels responsive, (2) matches industry standard (Alacritty, iTerm2, WezTerm all use 100-200ms), (3) eliminates resize storms at typical 60-120Hz drag event rates, (4) particularly beneficial for Windows ConPTY which has higher per-resize overhead. Documented in SPEC.md under Terminal resize section with pseudocode implementation pattern.
title: Decision: PTY resize debouncing

close_reason: All 3 sub-tasks delivered in a single Rust binary (console/src/main.rs): portable-pty PTY, vt100 screen parsing, ratatui rendering, keyboard input forwarding, bd create integration. Builds cleanly on Windows.

description: Minimal Rust binary: open a PTY with portable-pty, spawn claude as child process, pipe output through vt100::Parser to build a Screen, render the Screen in a ratatui widget. Target: 24 rows x 80 cols.
notes: COMPLETED: console/Cargo.toml and console/src/main.rs. Opens 24x80 PTY via portable-pty, spawns claude, feeds output through vt100::Parser, renders vt100::Screen in a ratatui widget, forwards keyboard input to PTY master. Ctrl+Q exits. Builds clean (no warnings) on Windows with portable-pty 0.8.1, vt100 0.15.2, ratatui 0.29.0, crossterm 0.28.1.

close_reason: Completed: Updated SPEC.md to remove all planner/headless-planner references. Replaced with direct orchestrator task decomposition model: renamed Planning section to Task Decomposition, removed plan action from tool table, updated task lifecycle flows, ticker messages, orchestrator view events (PLAN -> DECOMPOSE), prompt history examples, and config comments.

close_reason: Completed: headless planner agent spawns claude -p in background thread. Complex prompts (>15 words) routed via PlanRequest WsEvent. Planner output written to .dispatch/tasks.md, parsed for dependencies, and unblocked tasks dispatched to available agents. Falls back to direct dispatch on failure. Plan task completion triggers re-scan for newly unblocked tasks. 2 WS tests for routing.
description: When a complex voice prompt arrives, spawn a headless agent (no pane, no slot) that decomposes the task into subtasks with dependencies and writes the plan to .dispatch/tasks.md in the target repo. Planner exits after writing. Console reads the plan and begins dispatching workers. Simple one-off prompts skip planning. Agent instructions are embedded in the console binary (docs/AGENTS.md) and injected into the prompt.
title: Headless planner agent for task decomposition

These must be disabled when in input mode (arrow keys should pass through to the PTY in input mode, which is already the case since input mode forwards all non-Esc keys). notes: COMPLETED: Added .cargo/config.toml with lld linker config for MSVC/Linux targets (GNU commented out, needs lld installed). Added Cargo.toml profile tweaks: dev profile has incremental=true + line-tables-only debug info, release profile has thin LTO + strip. codegen-units defaults are already optimal. title: Investigate ways to make cargo build faster

close_reason: Fixed: orchestrator was spawned synchronously on first voice message, blocking the main thread. Moved spawn to a background thread at app startup with a pending_voice queue for messages arriving before orchestrator is ready.
description: There is noticeable lag when the phone sends the first voice message to the console. Likely caused by lazy initialization of the orchestrator (spawning the claude process on first transcript) or WebSocket connection setup. The first message should feel as responsive as subsequent ones.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: orchestrator::spawn() was called synchronously in the main event loop on first voice message, blocking for process startup + up to 10s waiting for session_id. FIX: moved orchestrator spawn to a background thread at app startup so it warms up before first voice input. Added pending_voice queue for messages that arrive before orchestrator is ready. REGRESSION TEST: no (comment says don't run cargo build/test/check).
title: Lag on first voice message from phone to console

close_reason: Completed: persistent LLM orchestrator replacing command parser. Added orchestrator.rs (spawn, message passing, tool call parsing), simplified ws_server to single VoiceTranscript event, radio sends raw transcripts, orchestrator view and status in header bar. Updated SPEC.md, README.md, ORCHESTRATOR.md, added CHANGELOG.md. All 35 tests pass.
description: Replace the keyword-based voice command parser with a persistent headless claude-code process that acts as the orchestrator. On startup, dispatch spawns the orchestrator with a system prompt containing available repos, tools, and current state. Voice transcripts from the phone are piped directly into the orchestrator as user messages. The orchestrator decides what to do via tool calls: dispatch agents, terminate agents, merge tasks, etc. This eliminates the command parser on both radio and console sides. Console becomes a thin runtime: TUI + PTY management + tool execution.
title: Persistent LLM orchestrator replacing command parser

close_reason: Completed: removed VoiceCommandParser, CommandParser, Command sealed class, and all tests. handleTranscript() now sends raw transcript as {"type":"transcript"} over WebSocket. Radio is a simple push-to-talk transcription device.
description: Remove VoiceCommandParser and intent-based command parsing from the Android radio app. Radio should send raw voice transcripts over WebSocket to the console, which pipes them to the orchestrator. Radio becomes a simple push-to-talk transcription device.
title: Strip command parser from radio app

description: Create docs/ORCHESTRATOR.md documenting the persistent LLM orchestrator: its system prompt, available tools, how it receives voice transcripts, decision-making responsibilities (dispatch, plan, terminate, merge), state awareness (repos, running agents, task status), and how it communicates with the console runtime.
title: Create ORCHESTRATOR.md defining orchestrator behavior

close_reason: Fixed: dispatch tool lacked a callsign parameter, so the orchestrator could not honor user-requested callsigns. Added optional callsign field to the Dispatch tool call and slot selection logic that prefers the matching NATO slot. Updated orchestrator prompt to instruct using the callsign parameter when addressing agents by name.
description: When the user asks to dispatch a specific callsign (e.g. 'dispatch Delta'), the orchestrator assigns whatever slot is next available instead of honoring the request. It then explains that callsigns are assigned automatically and refuses to use the requested one. The dispatch tool should accept an optional callsign/slot parameter, and the orchestrator prompt should instruct it to honor user-requested callsigns by dispatching to an available slot with that callsign.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: dispatch tool had no callsign parameter; orchestrator could not specify which agent to dispatch. FIX: Added optional callsign field to Dispatch tool call, slot selection logic that prefers the matching NATO slot, and updated orchestrator prompt to use the callsign parameter. REGRESSION TEST: Added parse_dispatch_call_with_callsign unit test.
title: Orchestrator ignores requested callsign when dispatching agent

close_reason: Completed: Esc immediately exits input mode. Double-Esc (within 300ms) sends literal escape to PTY via timestamp-based detection in command mode. Updated help overlay, SPEC.md, and README.md.
description: Currently in input mode, pressing Esc sets a flag and waits for the next keypress -- if the next key is Esc, a literal escape is sent to the PTY; if it's any other key, it exits to command mode (consuming that key). Change this so a single Esc press immediately returns to command mode. Double-Esc to send a literal Escape to the PTY should still work (exit and re-enter, or find an alternative mechanism). Update help overlay and SPEC.md.
title: Make Esc immediately exit input mode

close_reason: Completed: multi-repo support. Dispatch detects non-repo parent dirs, scans children for git repos, holds list in memory with rescan via S key. RepoSelect overlay for picking target repo. Per-slot repo_root for task/worktree ops. No artifacts at parent level. Single-repo mode backwards compatible.
description: Dispatch should detect when launched from a non-repo directory, scan immediate children for git repos, and hold the list in memory. Re-scan as needed. No .dispatch or workspace-level artifacts created at the parent level. If launched inside a git repo, behave as today (single-repo mode, backwards compatible).
notes: COMPLETED: multi-repo detection, Workspace enum, RepoSelect overlay, per-slot repo_root, rescan, header indicator, help/SPEC/README updates. DECISIONS: Workspace::MultiRepo holds Vec<String> of repo paths. In multi-repo mode, startup auto-dispatch is skipped. 'n' opens RepoSelect instead of dispatching directly. All task/worktree ops use per-slot repo_root.
title: Multi-repo support: launch dispatch from non-repo parent directory

close_reason: Fixed: merge tool handler was unconditionally returning success without validating agent work. Now checks (1) task branch doesn't still exist (agent merged), (2) merge commit exists on main (agent made changes). Returns success:false with diagnostic message when validation fails.
description: The orchestrator goes through the full dispatch-merge-conflict-resolve cycle (dispatching agents, merging tasks, even resolving merge conflicts), reporting success at each step, but no actual code changes end up on main and nothing is pushed. The merge tool returns 'ok' and the orchestrator proceeds as if work was done, but the agents may not have actually committed any changes in their worktrees. The entire dispatch/merge flow appears to be a no-op while the orchestrator believes it completed successfully.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: Merge tool handler unconditionally returned success:true without validating agent work. FIX: Added git-based validation checking (1) task branch still exists (agent failed to merge), (2) no merge commit found (agent did nothing), (3) success only when branch gone AND merge commit exists. REGRESSION TEST: no (comment says don't run cargo test).
title: Orchestrator reports successful merges and conflict resolution but no actual changes are made

close_reason: Completed: implemented orchestrator tool interface with 7 tools (dispatch, terminate, merge, list_agents, list_repos, plan, message_agent) in tools.rs, execution logic in App::execute_tool, JSON schema definitions for LLM tool-calling, text parsing, and 13 unit tests.
description: Define and implement the tools available to the orchestrator agent: dispatch(repo, prompt), terminate(agent), merge(task_id), list_agents(), list_repos(), plan(repo, prompt), message_agent(agent, text). Console executes these tool calls and returns results to the orchestrator.
title: Orchestrator tool interface

close_reason: Completed: git worktree-per-task isolation. Each task gets .dispatch/.worktrees/{id} on task/{id} branch. PTY launches in worktree dir. On completion: merge+cleanup. On conflict: ticker notice + preserve worktree. On termination: worktree preserved for reassignment.
description: Each task runs in an isolated git worktree under .dispatch/.worktrees/. On assignment: git worktree add .dispatch/.worktrees/{id} -b task/{id}. Agent PTY launches in the worktree directory. On completion: merge branch to main, clean up worktree. On conflict: flag on ticker, preserve worktree. On agent termination: preserve worktree and branch for reassignment.
title: Git worktree-per-task isolation

close_reason: Completed: Fixed psk_display() to return censored PSK (first 4 chars + '...') when psk_expanded is false, and full PSK when expanded. PSK is censored by default.
description: The 'p' hotkey toggles psk_expanded but psk_display() (line 467) always returns the full PSK regardless of the flag. Fix psk_display() to return a censored/masked value (e.g., '********' or first few chars + '...') when psk_expanded is false. PSK should be censored by default (psk_expanded starts as false, which is already correct at line 361). Update SPEC.md if needed.
title: Fix PSK visibility toggle ('p') - censor by default
